segments.title: Study Session Ready Confirmations — SPD Parking Follow-Up and iSol HR Scope
segments.summary: Quick confirmations on items 10-26. Eric Annis (chief development officer) follows up on the alternative parking location (1320 West Fayette) for SPD 1153 move: the site is 1300 ft from the building vs 850 ft for the pump track, has a narrow parcel limiting parking capacity, and lacks Westside Trail connectivity. SPD previously evaluated and rejected it. Brief question on whether iSol/Applicant Pro (item 10) covers fire and police; clarification deferred to HR. Councilor Ehrenreich notes she will abstain from item 24 (Cornell Cooperative camp) as her children are enrolled.
processing_notes: SPD and Fire department detections from HR scope question about iSol coverage
Department of Permits and Development detection is false positive from development officer reference
1320 West Fayette was the alternative parking site Councilor Caldwell suggested at Feb 18 session

segments.title: Senior Exemption Held for Data Analysis, Donna Reese Sign Dedication Announced
segments.summary: Patrona Jones-Rowser presents items 36-39. Item 36 withdrawn, 37 ready, 38 held for public hearing. Item 39 (senior tax exemption expansion) held after discussion with Assessment Commissioner Matt OJ who explains the income threshold decrease would expand the eligible pool by approximately 10-20 taxpayers, with larger benefits accruing to those making less. Council requests quantification data before voting. Donna Reese honorary street sign dedication announced for Friday Feb 27 at 1pm at West Brighton and Webster.
processing_notes: $4,000 refers to approximate annual income threshold change in senior exemption
Matt OJ surname partially transcribed; Assessment Commissioner
Donna Reese sign dedication continues from prior session planning

agenda_descriptions: Authorize ~ A Tax Exemption Agreement with a Housing Development Fund

Corporation to be known as Parkside Commons Phase 1 Housing Development
Fund Corporation, pursuant to Section 573 and Section 577 of Article 11 of the New
York State Private Housing Finance Law and Section 402 of the New York State Not-
For-Profit Corporation Law, for the properties located at 1935 ...

Approve — An installation of an Honorary Street Sign at the intersection Salt Springs
Road and Springfield Road, designating the section of Springfield Road on the west
end of LeMoyne College’s campus as “Dr. Linda LeMura Lane.” Dr.

Adopt — A City-wide six (6) month moratorium (draft attached) on Battery Energy
Storage Systems (BESS), regarding the issuance of permits and/or approvals related
to BESS facilities within the City of Syracuse.

segments.title: Items 40-45 — Parkside Held, BESS and Permits Ready
segments.summary: Jimmy Monto confirms items 40-45. Items 40-41 (Parkside Commons) held again. Item 42 (Dr. LeMura honorary sign) ready. Items 43 (BESS moratorium), 44 (Pond Street special use permit), and 45 ready. Brief coordination on waiver for item 46 (EV charging JCOM agreement) between Councilors Majok and Nave.
processing_notes: Parkside Commons items 40-41 held for fourth consecutive session

detected_departments: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
segments.title: EV Charging JCOM Agreement Approved — MWBE Language and Revenue Model Clarified
segments.summary: Council advances item 46: JCOM agreement for EV charging grant applications at Progress Park. Commissioner Michael Collins explains change from 3 to 2 chargers for faster Level 3 service; site chosen because National Grid recently upgraded the substation. Council secures commitment for MBE/WBE local subcontractor language in future contract. Joe Barry (first assistant corporation counsel) confirms ordinance is contingent on grant receipt with no binding contract with JCOM until grants are awarded. Karina Freeland (sustainability planner) details: no city staff time or match required (median household income qualifies), all costs covered by DEC and National Grid grants, 7.5% networking fee, city controls pricing and receives revenue. First 5 years maintenance with JCOM, city free to contract anyone after. Also: Richard Osber (HR) confirms iSol/Applicant Pro covers all departments citywide (fire and police use civil service list for sworn positions but civilian positions eligible). Council requests executive session on Good Cause Eviction legal specifics.

political_signal.description: Council approves EV charging opportunity after multi-session RFP debate, securing MWBE commitments as condition of support

processing_notes: EV charging debate resolved after 4 study sessions (Feb 4, 9, 18, 23)
JCOM has won 49 of 50 grant applications
Progress Park chosen because substation recently upgraded by National Grid
Ken Tsley (city electrical engineer) confirmed internal staff cannot complete required technical analysis
DEC funds charger equipment, National Grid funds site preparation (Battery Ready Program)
Parks and Fire department detections are false positives from unrelated discussion context
Breanna Stewart 3-on-3 basketball challenge joke from Councilor Caldwell

departments_found: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
